data.title: Annovis Bio Prices Registered Direct Offering to Fund Trials
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Annovis Bio ( (ANVS) ) is now available.

On April 9, 2026, Annovis Bio priced an underwritten registered direct offering of 5,263,156 shares of common stock, together with accompanying warrants to purchase an equal number of shares, at a combined price of $1.90 per share and warrant. The warrants, exercisable six months after issuance at $2.50 per share and expiring five and a half years later, are expected to bring the company gross proceeds of about $10 million from the stock and warrant sale, with the offering scheduled to close on or about April 10, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

All securities in the deal are being sold by Annovis, with Canaccord Genuity acting as sole bookrunner. The company plans to use the net proceeds primarily to fund the continued Phase 3 clinical development of buntanetap for Alzheimer’s disease, as well as for working capital and general corporate purposes, underscoring its strategy to advance its lead asset in a competitive neurodegenerative disease market.

The most recent analyst rating on (ANVS) stock is a Hold with a $1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Annovis Bio stock, see the ANVS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ANVS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ANVS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (no revenue, persistent losses, and worsening cash burn) and bearish technicals (price below major moving averages with negative MACD). A positive Phase 3 safety update provides some support, but long timelines to efficacy readouts and a lack of earnings-based valuation anchor keep the overall score low.

To see Spark’s full report on ANVS stock, click here.

More about Annovis Bio

Annovis Bio, Inc. is a Phase 3 clinical-stage biotechnology company headquartered in Malvern, Pa., focused on developing treatments for neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s. Its lead investigational product, buntanetap, is an oral therapy that targets multiple neurotoxic proteins via an RNA-based mechanism to potentially halt disease progression and improve cognitive and motor function.

Average Trading Volume: 568,342

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $45.94M

For a thorough assessment of ANVS stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Snail Amends ARK License, Outsources Project Aether Development
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Snail, Inc. Class A ( (SNAL) ) is now available.

On April 6, 2026, Snail, Inc. amended its exclusive license with SDE Inc. for ARK: Survival Evolved and ARK: Survival Ascended, effective April 1, 2026, cutting monthly licensing fees from $2 million to $1.5 million until the release of ARK 2 and revising DLC payments to $5 million only for certain new downloadable content released after October 1, 2023. These changes, involving entities controlled by insiders Ying Zhou and Hai Shi, are likely to lower ongoing royalty burdens and refine cash outflows tied to future content, potentially improving Snail’s cost structure around its flagship ARK franchise.

Also on April 6, 2026, Snail entered into a Software Development Outsourcing Agreement with Suzhou Snail Digital Technology to handle technical development for its Project Aether game, while retaining all intellectual property rights. The company committed to pay Suzhou Snail $1.966 million in four quarterly installments starting in the second quarter of 2026, underscoring its continued reliance on related-party development resources to advance its pipeline while preserving ownership of future game assets.

The most recent analyst rating on (SNAL) stock is a Sell with a $0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Snail, Inc. Class A stock, see the SNAL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SNAL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SNAL is a Underperform.

The score is weighed down primarily by severely distressed financials (revenue collapse, negative free cash flow, and negative equity with very large debt). Technicals remain bearish with the stock below key moving averages and negative MACD, while earnings-call positives (bookings/engagement and upcoming releases) are not enough to offset ongoing losses and Nasdaq compliance risks.

To see Spark’s full report on SNAL stock, click here.

More about Snail, Inc. Class A

Snail, Inc., through its wholly owned subsidiary Snail Games USA, operates in the video game industry, focusing on publishing and selling titles such as ARK: Survival Evolved and ARK: Survival Ascended under an exclusive worldwide software license. The company also invests in new game development projects, including Project Aether, while relying on related-party entities for licensing and outsourced development work.

Average Trading Volume: 30,366

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $16.42M

data.title: Top Executive Quietly Unloads Shares in Hello Group, Sparking Fresh Investor Buzz
data.description:
New insider activity at Hello Group ( (MOMO) ) has taken place on April 9, 2026.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hello Group’s Chief Technology Officer, Jianhua Wen, recently sold 4,218 shares of the company’s stock in a transaction valued at $24,042, according to the latest disclosed filing. This insider move provides a fresh data point for investors tracking executive trading activity at the firm.

Recent Updates on MOMO stock

Hello Group’s stock reacted to Q4 earnings that showed strong overseas growth, margin discipline, and improving user trends at Momo, but also deepening domestic revenue declines and regulatory pressure on value‑added services. Analysts updating price targets focused on this shift: they weighed the promise of fast‑growing, AI‑enhanced international and Tantan businesses and ongoing cash returns against persistent China weakness, tax scrutiny on live‑streaming, and guidance for lower group revenue and pressured margins in 2026.

Revisions were driven less by short‑term beats and more by a reassessment of the company’s risk‑reward profile as it transitions from a China‑centric live‑streaming model to a diversified global portfolio that is still loss‑making overseas. Management’s guidance for mid‑ to high‑teen mainland revenue declines, increased marketing to scale new platforms, and the time needed for Yaha Live and Amar to reach profitability led analysts to incorporate greater execution and macro uncertainty into their outlook, even as they acknowledged improving product quality, cost control, and a clear capital allocation strategy.

Spark’s Take on MOMO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MOMO is a Neutral.

The score is supported primarily by balance-sheet strength and a reasonable valuation, but is held back by weakening fundamentals (shrinking revenue, margin compression, softer cash conversion) and bearish technicals. The latest earnings call reinforces the near-term risk: management expects further revenue declines in 2026 even as overseas growth and cost discipline partially offset domestic pressure.

To see Spark’s full report on MOMO stock, click here.
